title: Find Largest Value in Each Tree Row
content:
Given the root of a binary tree, return an array of the largest value in each row of the tree (0-indexed).

Example 1:

Input: root = [1,3,2,5,3,null,9]
Output: [1,3,9]

Example 2:

Input: root = [1,2,3]
Output: [1,3]

Constraints:

• The number of nodes in the tree will be in the range [0, 104].

• -231 <= Node.val <= 231 - 1

codeSnippets.code:
# Definition for a binary tree node.
# class TreeNode(object):
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.left = left
#         self.right = right

        """
        :type root: Optional[TreeNode]
        :rtype: List[int]
        """

solutions.explanation: Use BFS to traverse level by level. For each level, track the maximum value and add it to result.
solutions.complexity: Time: O(n) - visit each node once. Space: O(w) where w is max width.
